name: United States (1867–1877)
description: Thirteen alternating red and white horizontal stripes (seven red, six white). In the upper hoist, a blue canton bearing 37 white five-pointed stars in five rows of 8-7-7-7-8.
history: Adopted on 4 July 1867 following the admission of Nebraska as the 37th state on 1 March 1867. Sometimes called the 'Nebraska flag', it served as the official flag for ten years until 4 July 1877, when Colorado's admission brought the count to 38.

features.symbolism: the union of the states; a new constellation
the 37 states of the Union as of 4 July 1867
